priorities: Defensive large‑cap equity exposure, especially in healthcare/pharma
Capital deployment to accelerate neuro‑degenerative research
Operational precision and automation in industrial assets
redLines: Divest any holding that reduces or abandons Parkinson’s/brain‑disease research funding
Avoid companies that chase short‑term lifestyle profits at the expense of core R&D
preferredSectors: Large‑cap pharmaceuticals & biotech with neuroscience pipelines
avoidedSectors: Consumer lifestyle sectors lacking substantive R&D
Companies with weak vertical integration or low automation
esgFocus: Industrial sustainability through automation and precision

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Multi‑year to generational, with an urgent medium‑term focus on funding a cure for his daughter
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Primarily stable, low‑turnover assets; limited liquidity allocated to foundation endowments and targeted biotech opportunities

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe was studying abroad; family situation stable.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: No immediate change; reaffirmed long‑term wealth preservation focus.

lifeEvents.description: Discussion of long‑term wealth transmission for children; 12% allocation to global healthcare giants.
Requested detailed breakdown of pharma holdings, specifically neurological research footprints.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Increased scrutiny of pharma assets; early sign of health‑focused tilt.

lifeEvents.description: Noted corporate capital tied up in physical assets; preference for large‑cap equities with heavy R&D budgets.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Continued emphasis on stable, research‑intensive equities.

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe diagnosed with rapidly progressing early‑onset Parkinson’s disease.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Shift from pure wealth preservation to aggressive health‑impact investing; emotional mandate to fund cure.

lifeEvents.description: Family established a Venture Philanthropy foundation dedicated to neuro‑degenerative research.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Allocation of private wealth to foundation; focus on pharma/biotech that support Parkinson’s research.

lifeEvents.description: Requested audit of core European pharma holdings’ neuro‑degenerative clinical pipelines.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Ensuring existing holdings align with the new health‑impact mandate.

lifeEvents.description: Stated any company abandoning Parkinson’s research would be a personal betrayal; demand immediate divestment.
Transferred CHF 1 million from personal portfolio to family foundation as an immediate grant to a neuro‑genetics lab.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Direct capital deployment to targeted research; reduces liquid assets.

lifeEvents.description: Attended medical charity gala; emphasized need for continuous capital flow to leading neuroscience companies.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforces commitment to health‑focused equity exposure.

lifeEvents.description: Met biotech firm with breakthrough Parkinson’s asset; reiterated that large‑cap holdings must financially support the fight.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Potential tilt toward selective biotech exposure while maintaining large‑cap defensive core.

interests: Arts and cultural philanthropy (via wife Carmen)
quirks: Annual week‑long offline ski trips for decision‑making
Regular long‑distance cycling rides for mental clarity
Hands‑on involvement in factory automation projects
avoidTopics: Consumer lifestyle brands focused on short‑term profit
Political debates unrelated to business or health
Any discussion implying reduced R&D funding for health

keyQuotes.quote: My business runs on 5-year OEM supply agreements. My portfolio runs on the same principle. Patience and precision.
Transparency in manufacturing is the foundation of long‑term OEM trust.
Our private wealth is now a weapon to save Chloe. I want our core healthcare and pharma holdings to actively support the entities pushing the boundaries of brain disease research.
If a company we own ever abandons or defunds Parkinson's research to chase cheap lifestyle profits, it would feel like a personal betrayal of my daughter. Flag them immediately for divestment.
Factory automation update — our third production line in Zug is now fully robotic for body component fabrication. 18‑month ROI projected.
